confirmed_cases.description: Healthcare worker, earliest known suspected case. Developed symptoms. Died approximately three days later at a medical center in Bunia.
Confirmed via INRB Kinshasa Bundibugyo-specific assay. Part of batch of 13 samples — 8 tested positive.
Healthcare worker. Confirmed in same INRB batch.
Confirmed in INRB batch. Epidemiological link to Mongbwalu cases unclear.
Healthcare worker confirmed in INRB batch. Treated patients from Mongbwalu.
Community member. Confirmed in INRB batch.
Healthcare worker. Fourth HCW confirmed in initial batch.
Community member confirmed in INRB batch.
Confirmed after DRC official outbreak declaration. Contact of case 5.
Community member confirmed after outbreak declaration.
First Uganda case. Traveler from DRC, lab-confirmed in Kampala.
Second Uganda case. Traveler from DRC, no apparent epi link to case 11. One death.
Wife of a man who died of Ebola in Bunia. Traveled from Ituri to Goma (1M+ population, M23-controlled). Most alarming geographic escalation.
Confirmed case in the DRC capital, ~1,000 km from Ituri epicenter. Long-distance importation.

response_timeline.event: First suspected case develops symptoms (HCW, Mongbwalu)
Index case dies at medical center in Bunia
WHO alerted to high-mortality unknown illness in Mongbwalu
Rapid response team dispatched to investigate
Investigation completed by rapid response team
INRB confirms Bundibugyo ebolavirus (8/13 samples positive)
DRC officially declares Ebola outbreak
Africa CDC issues continental alert
First Uganda case confirmed in Kampala
Second Uganda case confirmed; one death
Africa CDC convenes high-level meeting (130+ participants)
WHO declares PHEIC
Confirmed case reported in Goma (North Kivu, 1M+ pop.)
Confirmed case in Kinshasa (~1,000 km from epicenter)
6 Americans exposed in DRC, 1 symptomatic
US Embassy Juba issues health alert for South Sudan
Rwanda closes Rubavu-Goma border indefinitely
US enhanced screening at ports of entry
Field hospitals established in Bunia (facilities overwhelmed)
US Embassy Kampala issues health alert for Uganda
US invokes Title 42: 30-day entry ban on non-US nationals recently in DRC/Uganda/South Sudan (first US Ebola-specific travel ban)
WHO IHR Emergency Committee holds first meeting in Geneva
WHO DG briefing: ~600 suspected, 139 suspected deaths; national risk upgraded to VERY HIGH
American physician Dr. Peter Stafford (Bundibugyo-positive) medevac'd from DRC to Charite Berlin
First confirmed Ebola case in South Kivu Province (M23-held territory near Bukavu); patient died before diagnosis confirmed
India-Africa Forum Summit (New Delhi) postponed by AU and India
US-bound flights from Ebola-affected region funneled to Dulles, Atlanta, Houston for CDC screening
Community protests in Rwampara HZ; police fire tear gas; two treatment tents burned
WHO IHR Emergency Committee issues first temporary recommendations; national risk VERY HIGH, regional HIGH, global LOW
WHO R&D Blueprint recommends two monoclonal antibodies + obeldesivir for prioritized Bundibugyo clinical trials
Second American (high-risk contact) transferred to Czech Republic for monitoring
Uganda confirms 3 new Ebola cases (driver, HCW, DRC traveler); Uganda total now 5
Africa CDC warns 10 additional countries at risk: Angola, Burundi, CAR, Rep. Congo, Ethiopia, Kenya, Rwanda, South Sudan, Tanzania, Zambia
Second MSF treatment tent burned in Mongbwalu; 18 suspected patients flee into community
Three Red Cross volunteers revealed to have died May 5-16 after contracting Ebola during body management on March 27 — pre-declaration transmission
DRC confirmed cases surpass 100 (101 confirmed, 904 suspected); WHO national risk VERY HIGH
US demands DRC World Cup team maintain 21-day isolation bubble in Belgium before US entry
ECDC updates: DRC 101 confirmed + 904 suspected, Uganda 7 confirmed; combined 108 confirmed, 11 deaths confirmed
DRC Ministry of Health reports 121 confirmed (incl. 17 deaths) and 1,077 suspected cases (incl. 238 deaths) across Ituri, North Kivu and South Kivu
Uganda orders immediate closure of its border with DRC for at least four weeks; emergency arrivals face mandatory 21-day isolation (against WHO advice)
ECDC scales up its response via the EU Health Task Force as the outbreak intensifies; EU/EEA risk remains very low
DRC Ministry of Health revises figures: 125 confirmed (17 deaths) and 906 suspected (223 deaths) — non-cases removed and some suspected reclassified as confirmed
WHO Director-General travels to the DRC to support the outbreak response
WHO DON605: 134 confirmed cases (18 deaths) across DRC and Uganda; cases span 13 health zones (7 Ituri, 5 North Kivu, 1 South Kivu)
